questions.q: Is this an autonomous agent execution system?
questions.a: Not yet. It is intentionally a safety-first request control layer. Approval, execution, and automation are blocked until reviewed phases.

questions.q: Does it expose secrets in the browser?
questions.a: No. The browser calls Netlify Functions. Service role and secret keys are not exposed to browser code.
The harness and execution plan are ready. Manual live testing with a real token is a gated step before full external rollout.

questions.q: What is the strongest technical proof point?
questions.a: It has progressed from local lifecycle scaffolding through Supabase Auth/RLS, real authenticated reads, controlled writes, lifecycle event creation, safe errors, operator workspace, and demo package.
Request update/delete, approvals, execution, automation, deploy/merge/push/PR controls, env mutation, migrations, and browser service-role access.
